title: Implement and Use a Custom Exception

content.problemStatement:
You're building a library system that allows users to borrow books. However, a user **cannot borrow more than 3 books at a time**. If they try to do so, a custom exception `LimitExceededException` should be thrown.

Requirements
Define a custom checked exception `LimitExceededException` that extends `Exception`.
Create a class `LibraryUser` with:

A `String name`
An `int borrowedBooks`
A method `borrowBook()` that:

Increments the number of borrowed books
Throws `LimitExceededException` if the user tries to borrow more than 3 books





Input Format

First line: `String` representing the user’s name
Second line: `int` representing the number of books the user wants to borrow


Output Format

Print `"Book borrowed"` for each successful borrow.
If the limit is exceeded, print `"LimitExceededException: Cannot borrow more than 3 books"` (for each extra borrow).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of users. For each user, two lines follow: a string Name (may contain spaces) representing the user's name, and an integer N (0 ≤ N ≤ 10) representing how many books the user attempts to borrow.
content.outputFormat: For each borrow attempt, print a line: "Book borrowed" if the borrow succeeds, otherwise "LimitExceededException: Cannot borrow more than 3 books" for every attempt beyond the third. Lines are printed in the order the attempts are processed across all users.

sampleTestCases.output:
Book borrowed
Book borrowed
Book borrowed
LimitExceededException: Cannot borrow more than 3 books
sampleTestCases.explanation: Alice attempts to borrow 4 books. The first three succeed, the fourth exceeds the limit and triggers the exception message.

sampleTestCases.output:
Book borrowed
Book borrowed
Book borrowed
Book borrowed
Book borrowed
LimitExceededException: Cannot borrow more than 3 books
LimitExceededException: Cannot borrow more than 3 books
sampleTestCases.explanation: Bob borrows 2 books – both succeed. Carol attempts 5 books – the first three succeed, the last two exceed the limit and each produce the exception line.
Dave does not attempt any borrow, so no output is produced.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput:
Book borrowed
Book borrowed
Book borrowed
LimitExceededException: Cannot borrow more than 3 books
LimitExceededException: Cannot borrow more than 3 books
LimitExceededException: Cannot borrow more than 3 books
LimitExceededException: Cannot borrow more than 3 books
LimitExceededException: Cannot borrow more than 3 books
LimitExceededException: Cannot borrow more than 3 books
LimitExceededException: Cannot borrow more than 3 books

Book borrowed
Book borrowed
Book borrowed

hidden.label: multiple users mixed limits
hidden.expectedOutput:
Book borrowed
Book borrowed
Book borrowed
Book borrowed
Book borrowed
LimitExceededException: Cannot borrow more than 3 books
Book borrowed

hidden.label: all users exceed limit
hidden.expectedOutput:
Book borrowed
Book borrowed
Book borrowed
LimitExceededException: Cannot borrow more than 3 books
LimitExceededException: Cannot borrow more than 3 books
LimitExceededException: Cannot borrow more than 3 books
Book borrowed
Book borrowed
Book borrowed
LimitExceededException: Cannot borrow more than 3 books

solution.approach: Iterate over each test case. Keep a counter of books already borrowed for the current user. For each of the N attempts, if the counter is less than 3, increment it and output "Book borrowed"; otherwise output the exception message. The custom exception is only needed for the Java solution to demonstrate checked exception handling, but the core logic is identical for all languages.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total number of borrow attempts) — at most T*10 operations.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — only a few integer variables are used.
